In the last 5 years, a total of 33 SIPCOT Industrial Parks were launched, creating 31 lakh jobs. 50 new SIPCOT Industrial Parks will be launched in the next 5 years, covering areas that require additional industrial growth.

points.section_title: Micro, Small and Medium Scale Enterprises (MSMEs)
points.text: New Industrial Estates will be created in Chengalpattu, Kancheepuram, and Tiruvallur districts along the Chennai Peripheral Ring Road (CPRR) to enhance export opportunities.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Micro, Small and Medium Scale Enterprises (MSMEs)

points.text: New electric crematoriums will be established in Municipal Corporations and Municipalities. Residents from neighbouring villages will also be permitted to utilise these facilities.

Subsidies and necessary training will be provided to rural youth, registered farmers' associations, and Farmer-Producer Organisations (FPOs) to establish 100 agricultural machinery repair and service centres.

Integrated Mental Health Development Centres will be established in all districts, with the appointment of psychiatrists and mental health counsellors. In today's fast-paced lifestyle, the general public faces various mental health challenges, including stress. While existing mental health hospitals and psychiatric departments primarily prioritise the treatment of mental illness, these new "Integrated Mental Health Development Centres" are designed to focus on Mental Health Promotion (Positive Mental Health), Stress Management Counselling, and Tele-counselling Services. Through these centres, individuals of all ages, from adolescents to the elderly, will receive guidance for mental well-being, stress management support, and access to telephone-based counselling services.
Schemes will be created to invite researchers from Tamil Nadu working across the world as 'Visiting Faculty' and utilize their expertise for the benefit of our students.
A special scheme will be formulated and implemented to increase the export of meat and fish to enhance the income of our farmers and fishermen.

points.section_title: Micro, Small and Medium Scale Enterprises (MSMEs)
points.text: Measures will be taken to transform Tamil Nadu from being a hub for raw coir exports into a global manufacturing hub for High-Value Coir Value-Added Products.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Micro, Small and Medium Scale Enterprises (MSMEs)

points.text: Due to the widespread urbanisation across the state, there is a growing demand for housing, commercial spaces for industries and businesses, and public areas. To meet these requirements, new regulations will be formulated by comparing the building policies, including FSI (Floor Space Index), of major cities across the country.
1,000 food value-addition centres will be established to enhance the processing of agricultural produce.
Palliative and Geriatric Care departments will be established in all government medical college hospitals.
An International Science Conference will be conducted once every two years with the participation of global scientists.

When industrial investments are attracted and jobs are created, the information regarding those opportunities must reach the job seekers. Simultaneously, a ‘Skill Matching Portal’ will be created by the Industries Department to connect companies seeking employees with the youth. Through this, companies can access details of the youth without an information gap, and job seekers can easily learn about the required skills in one place.

points.section_title: Micro, Small and Medium Scale Enterprises (MSMEs)
points.text: Integrated Value Chains will be created for Moringa, Chilli, Rice, Banana, Mango, Coconut, Marine Fish, Guava, Tomato, Cashew, and Groundnut by decentralizing production and strengthening processing infrastructure.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Micro, Small and Medium Scale Enterprises (MSMEs)

points.text: Considering the rapid population growth and the fast-paced development of suburban areas in major corporations like Trichy, Madurai, Coimbatore, and Salem, new 'Global Cities' with modern, world-class amenities will be developed in the vicinity of these corporations.

A 'Special Scheme' will be created to encourage the cultivation of medicinal plants. Cultivation of medicinal crops such as Coleus (Marunthukkurkan), Glory Lily (Senganthal), Tanner’s Cassia (Aavaram), and Periwinkle (Nithya Kalyani) will be undertaken across 25,000 acres.
A National History Seminar will be conducted once every two years with the participation of experts in history and archaeology.
A fund of Rs. 100 crore will be created to encourage Research and Development (R&D) in emerging Sunrise Sectors.

points.section_title: Micro, Small and Medium Scale Enterprises (MSMEs)
points.text: Infrastructure facilities will be created to benefit 500 MSMEs by encouraging Public-Private Partnerships (PPP) in High Value – Low Volume infrastructure.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: Micro, Small and Medium Scale Enterprises (MSMEs)

points.text: New Uzhavar Sandhais will be established in all Panchayat Union areas that currently do not have one.

To strengthen the creativity of students, sports, arts, and cultural festivals will be promoted annually in every college in Tamil Nadu. Festivals will also be conducted at district and state levels, and best contributors will be selected and awarded.
Desalination plants will be established in coastal areas to ensure sufficient water supply to industries.

The use of drones will be promoted to perform pesticide spraying and crop protection at a lower cost while ensuring higher yields. This initiative will be implemented through e-vaadagai scheme, making drones available to farmers at affordable rent. Furthermore, this scheme will provide drone pilot training, creating employment opportunities for the youth, with a particular focus on women.

Festivals will be conducted in all colleges to highlight the antiquity of Tamil, its evolution, and the continuous changes occurring in Tamil society. Such festivals will serve to mold our students as those who have internalized our history, the path to be traveled, and the moral values we must cherish as a society.
A Viability Gap Fund (VGF) will be created to set up and operate systems that convert dye effluent into clean water in textile cluster areas. This will ensure sustainable growth in those regions.

Similar to the successful operation of 'Uzhavar Sandhais' (Farmers' Markets) in rural areas, 100 'Uzhavar Angadis' (Farmers' Outlets) will be established in urban areas. These will be set up in the Corporation areas in the first phase and gradually expanded to Municipalities. This will ensure farmers receive fair prices for their produce, while consumers gain access to high-quality products at lower costs.
Changes will be made periodically in the training provided to teachers to enable them to internalize and teach students the new changes made in the curriculum according to the needs of the times.
Advanced Quality Testing Labs will be established to meet the requirements of the industrial sector.
To boost agricultural exports, "Agricultural Market Intelligence and Export Guidance Centres" equipped with AI technology will be established at both the zonal and state levels.
A scheme will be implemented to provide encouragement and assistance for a larger number of Tamil Nadu students to pass examinations such as Chartered Accountant (CA) and Company Secretary (CS).
An Additive Manufacturing and Metallurgical Lab will be established at Taramani, Chennai, in collaboration with IIT Madras.
To benefit the farming community, Farmer-Producer Organisations (FPOs), entrepreneurs, students, and the general public, Zonal Agricultural Exhibitions and Seminars will be conducted annually.

To ensure that our government university students participate in leading global research, special schemes will be implemented to encourage all our universities to sign agreements with foreign universities for reciprocal training of students and teachers.
10 Mega Plug and Play Parks will be established through SIPCOT, with all necessary infrastructure to enable factories to commence production immediately.
High-yielding crop varieties will be identified and produced through seed farms to ensure their availability.
Measures will be taken across all districts to increase the area of high-income horticultural crops such as Moringa, Guava, Papaya, Sapota, Jackfruit, Gooseberry, Jamun, Custard Apple, and Orange.
Special schemes will be formulated to increase the production of pulses and oilseeds.

Over the next five years, 5 Crore palmyra seeds will be planted. Special clusters will be established for palmyra-based products. Arrangements will also be made to market Palmyra products manufactured in Tamil Nadu through Aavin outlets.

points.title: New Agro Industrial Parks
points.text: In Tamil Nadu, certain products are produced in high concentration in a few areas and are largely sold without value addition. Dedicated Agro-Industrial Parks will be established across the State to address this. These Agro-Industrial Parks will focus on Eggs, Coconut, Banana, Mango, Paddy, and Flowers. These parks will be established in Pattukkottai, Krishnagiri, Udhagamandalam, Yercaud, Kodaikanal, Theni, Ramanathapuram, Tenkasi, Tiruvannamalai, Salem, Pollachi, Oddanchatram, and Thiruvarur.
Mobile paddy dryers will be provided as per local demands at direct paddy procurement centres operating in paddy-growing regions.

points.title: Silo Storage
points.text: Procuring paddy during seasons when the moisture content is high remains a major challenge. By establishing Silo Storage facilities, it will be possible to dry and procure large quantities of paddy even with 20% moisture content. Therefore, these grain storage silos will be constructed in regions with high paddy cultivation.

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights
points.title: 1 No-Application Scheme
points.text: Our every government scheme will reach the people directly and firmly. In that sense, our government will be the first in India to bring welfare schemes directly to the people without forcing any documents on them (No-Application). Today, people face various pressures like proving eligibility, correctly filling applications, contacting relevant offices, and giving bribes to officials to receive government schemes. This is merely a method to deceive people unfairly, not people-oriented politics. In this situation, within the next five to seven years, our TVK rule will advance Tamil Nadu as the 'first state in India to implement people's welfare schemes without forcing any documents (No-Application)'. Through digital information including Aadhaar, family cards, family details, and activities with the help of technology, we will identify persons eligible for welfare schemes. Eligible beneficiaries will receive the benefits of the schemes without any application procedure. Benefits of the scheme will be brought to the beneficiaries' doorsteps through 'Chief Minister's Public Service Friend'.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights
points.title: 2 Right to Service Act; Service to All on Time by Law
points.text: It now takes weeks for people to obtain basic government documents like birth certificates, land pattas, and caste certificates. To ensure people receive the benefits of 500 government services on time, an act will be enacted within a hundred days of the TVK government taking office.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 2 Right to Service Act; Service to All on Time by Law
A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights
points.title: 3 Vettri Tamil Nadu Super App; Government in Your Mobile
points.text: To prevent people from wandering to government offices for government services, everyone can receive 500 services including birth certificates, family cards, and driver's license renewal from their mobiles through the 'Vettri Tamil Nadu Super App'.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 3 Vettri Tamil Nadu Super App; Government in Your Mobile
A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights
points.title: 4 Non-discriminatory Service through Entitlement Cards
points.text: Like the Aadhaar card, an entitlement card will be provided to every family. Through this, all eligible families can easily receive benefits for their services. Through this, our government administration will be for everyone without any room for political interference.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights
points.title: 5 Tamil Nadu Governance Monitoring Platform
points.text: The TVK government will bring the 'Real Time Governance Dashboard' (Tamil Nadu Aluvval Kangaanippu Thalam) into operation. Through this, government income-expenditure accounts, the amount allocated for schemes for a constituency and district, complaints-questions raised by the public regarding them, and government answers will all be uploaded directly and immediately.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights
points.title: 1 People's Arena; Direct Complaints from People Debated for a Full Day in Assembly
points.text: A digital platform named 'Makkal Arangam' (People's Arena) will be created so that people can directly inform the government of their demands, bypassing their MLAs. People can directly register their complaints through this. *The government will consider demands registered with the signatures of 10,000 people. *Demands registered with the signatures of 5 lakh people will be taken up as a day's 'People's Arena' debate in the Assembly.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 1 People's Arena; Direct Complaints from People Debated for a Full Day in Assembly
A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights
points.title: 2 Vettri Tamil Nadu Platform
points.text: Through the 'Vettri Tamil Nadu Platform', everyone can give their ideas to the government, express acceptance or rejection of government schemes, and evaluate government services from time to time.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights
points.title: 3 People's Right to Choose; 10% Fund in District-level Expenditure Allocated to Schemes Desired by People
points.text: People will decide which schemes the 10% fund allocated for development projects in all districts should be assigned to. During the budget, this will be separately categorized as 'Participatory Budget' and projects will be undertaken. A committee of 10 people including women, youth, Scheduled Caste and Tribal people, and sectoral experts will be created in every district. Their task is to monitor and evaluate district expenditure and take people's demands to the government.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 3 People's Right to Choose; 10% Fund in District-level Expenditure Allocated to Schemes Desired by People
A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights
points.title: 1 Help Number for People Against Bribery
points.text: Bribery continues to run rampant because people cannot boldly complain against it. Against this, in TVK rule, complaint numbers will be pasted in the central part of all government offices. If people send complaints about officials demanding bribes to that number connected with the CM office, not only will the complaining people be given proper recognition, but they will also be protected through the State Whistleblower Protection Act.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 1 Help Number for People Against Bribery
A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights
points.title: 2 Strengthening Lokayukta
points.text: Lokayukta will be strengthened by providing powers to prosecute erring officials and elected people's representatives along with a transparent appointment method.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights
points.title: 3 Tamil Nadu Anti-Corruption Monitoring Wing; Bribery Prevented Before It Happens
points.text: Beyond taking action after a government malpractice has occurred, the technical-enabled anti-corruption monitoring wing in TVK rule will continuously monitor registers and money transfers related to procurement and welfare distribution to prevent malpractices before the crime happens.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 3 Tamil Nadu Anti-Corruption Monitoring Wing; Bribery Prevented Before It Happens
A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights
points.title: 4 Abolishing Sand Smuggling
points.text: The method of extracting minerals and sand will be allowed legally only through fair and transparent auctions.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights
points.title: 1 Drug-free Tamil Nadu
points.text: A narcotics control force will be created under the direct supervision of the Chief Minister. Through this, acting on a war footing, drug usage will be completely eradicated. Drug sellers will not only be arrested but also detained under the Goondas Act. An anti-drug club will be established in every school. The TVK government will create a 'Rehabilitation Fund' to help those recovering from addiction. Furthermore, a special fund of Rs. 10 lakh for infrastructure projects will be provided to villages that eradicate drugs, along with a government evaluation certificate.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights

points.subsection_title: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights
points.title: 2 Healthy Addiction-free Tamil Nadu
points.text: With the primary aim of protecting the welfare of women, families, and homes, the TVK government will follow a systematic and gradual approach to reduce liquor consumption. TVK will work to reduce the social and financial burdens caused by liquor through controlled reduction of liquor availability, strong addiction recovery schemes, and awareness led by women's groups. Specifically, TASMAC shops located near schools and temples will be immediately closed or ensured to be relocated, and a safe environment established.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: A. Protection of Tamil Nadu's Financial and State Rights

points.title: 3 Justice for Those Killed in Custodial Killings
points.text: Every custodial killing happening in a state shows the failure of the government administering that state. In our TVK rule, an independent investigation will be conducted to get justice for the families affected by custodial deaths conducted during the DMK rule. Strict action will be taken without any bias against everyone involved in such heinous violence.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 3 Justice for Those Killed in Custodial Killings

points.title: 4 Judicial Infrastructure Enhancement & Reducing Pending Cases
points.text: To reduce the number of pending cases in courts and make justice easily accessible to the public, new local courts will be established.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 4 Judicial Infrastructure Enhancement & Reducing Pending Cases

points.title: 5 Zero Tolerance for Illegal Liquor Sales
points.text: The TVK government is committed to fully eradicating the production and sale of fake, illegal, and black market liquor. Considering this a very serious crime, strict legal actions will be taken against the concerned sellers, suppliers, and accomplices.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 5 Zero Tolerance for Illegal Liquor Sales

points.title: 1 Five Special Budgets for the First Time in India
points.text: Starting from the announcement of funds for schemes under this budget to reaching the beneficiaries, the social impact caused by them will be subjected to intense monitoring and analysis. They are: *Social Justice Budget: This fund allocated for the equal progress of women, Scheduled Caste and Tribal people, and all sections of marginalized people, will be spent fully without any diversion. *Equal Regional Development Budget: Schemes for non-discriminatory development of all backward districts, including the 12 most backward districts, will be designed. *Tamil Nadu Cultural and Heritage Budget: Funds for reclaiming and protecting archaeological sites, Keezhadi excavation, and taking Tamil culture to the world stage. *Education and Medicine Equal Opportunity Budget: Eliminating quality differences between educational institutions and hospitals, filling vacancies, world-class education and medicine. *Ecology Budget: Coastal resource protection, greening arid districts, flood prevention management in cities, new agricultural methods, facing climate change.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 1 Five Special Budgets for the First Time in India

points.title: 1 We Will Transform Tamil Nadu into an Economically Sovereign State.
points.text: In the 2026-27 financial year, Tamil Nadu's debt exceeds Rs. 10.71 lakh crore. The functioning of the TVK government will not be about wasting money that should be spent on people. It will be about preventing losses, increasing revenue, and creating economic structures in a new way without imposing new taxes on Tamil Nadu people. TVK government will carry out revenue growth through modern methods like eradicating corruption, mobilizing Non-Resident Tamils to attract new investments, reviving MSMEs, technology, ecology, and new knowledge efficiency.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 1 We Will Transform Tamil Nadu into an Economically Sovereign State.

points.title: 2 Tamil Nadu Strategic Investment Authority (TNSIA)
points.text: Moving away from a method dependent only on taxes and subsidies, the TVK government will lead Tamil Nadu towards a new economic model that creates wealth. TVK government will establish a Strategic Investment Authority (TNSIA) with a value of Rs. 50,000 crore. Through this, the government will invest directly as a stakeholder in high-growth sectors like semiconductors, electric vehicles (EVs), aerospace, AI, and advanced manufacturing. Beyond just coordinating investments, the Tamil Nadu government in TVK rule will invest along with industrialists, create new values, and share their ownership. This will create lakhs of high-quality jobs along with bringing new non-tax revenue sources to the state.

points.title: 3 White Paper on State Financial Status and Debt Management
points.text: The TVK government will publish a comprehensive White Paper covering Tamil Nadu's financial status, debt levels, borrowing methods, revenue usage, and missed economic opportunities. This will serve as a foundation for transparent, responsible, and growth-oriented financial strategies.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: 3 White Paper on State Financial Status and Debt Management

points.title: 4 Public Sector Undertakings (PSUs) Recovery Project
points.text: The TVK government will implement structural reforms in all PSUs to improve operational efficiency, increase profit, and reduce financial burden. Furthermore, to strengthen administration, sector-specific experts will be appointed along with introducing professional management practices.

points.title: World-class Chennai, Trichy, Madurai, and Coimbatore Cities
points.text: Chennai, Trichy, Madurai, and Coimbatore will be created as world-class cities with sustainable future plans, planned growth to solve housing congestion, modern infrastructure, and improved life.

hierarchy_down_to_top.label: World-class Chennai, Trichy, Madurai, and Coimbatore Cities
